title: Market Brief — Thursday, April 30, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s at 85% and H100s at 70% availability signal oversupply in last-gen silicon, while B200s at 25% are scarce and GB200s at 10% availability indicate severe shortage in next-gen compute. The B200 OCPI jumped 58.1% annualized volatility with utilization surging 45.8pp in 30 days to 87.3%, the clearest demand signal in the stack.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s at 85% and H100s at 70% availability signal oversupply in last-gen silicon, while B200s at 25% are scarce and GB200s at 10% availability indicate severe shortage in next-gen compute. The B200 OCPI jumped 58.1% annualized volatility with utilization surging 45.8pp in 30 days to 87.3%, the clearest demand signal in the stack.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100s costs roughly $9.84/M tokens, meaning DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is selling at a loss of approximately $9.46 per million tokens — Burning Cash. DeepSeek R1 at $2.50/M output loses around $7.34/M on H100s. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output prints money at 91%+ margins on B200s (TCO ~$5.03/M), grading Strong, while Google's Gemini 2.5 Flash at $2.50/M output is Neutral at best on efficient hardware.

B200 compute at $4.20/hr spot with only 25% availability is undervalued given utilization rocketing to 87.3% — accumulate B200 capacity now before scarcity pricing kicks in. A100s at 85% availability and $0.90/hr are oversupplied legacy iron; sell or redeploy A100 commitments and rotate into premium-tier API providers capturing 83-95% margins on next-gen silicon.
bullets: 25% availability on B200s qualifies as scarce compute, down from abundant just 30 days ago as utilization surged 45.8 percentage points.
10% availability on GB200s signals severe shortage with no reliable spot pricing yet established.
$80/M output makes o3 Pro the most expensive API token on the market, roughly 212x costlier than DeepSeek V3.2.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token prices have fallen roughly 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline of 100.
$9.52/M TCO-adjusted cost on MI300X makes AMD the most expensive inference platform, 2.4x pricier than B200.
93.6% utilization on H100s at 70% availability suggests remaining idle capacity is largely reserved or committed inventory.
